title: Atelophragma purpusii
intro: Atelophragma purpusii, also known as Purpus' climbing fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Blechnaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of A. purpusii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional Mexican medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, with the dried leaves being a common ingredient in these herbal preparations.
taxonomy: Atelophragma purpusii is a species of frog in the family Bufonidae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of this species has not been extensively documented, but it is believed to be part of the Atelophragma genus, which is a group of small to medium-sized frogs found in the region.
